publicationStmt.publisher: Schenker Documents Online in association with the Centre for Computing in the Humanities

sourceDesc.p: No source: created in electronic format.

encodingDesc.p: Encoding according to the CCH TEI Guidelines

div.p: The first son of Reinhard Oppel and Gertrud Oppel (née Bandelow).

Ulrich was Reinhard Oppel’s third child, and the first of his second marriage, to Gertrude Bandelow, his younger siblings in that marriage being Walter and Hans Reinhard, all three being adversely affected by the death in 1925 of their mother.

Ulrich’s primary wish was to join the German Navy but he was rejected in 1930 and again in 1933, and instead trained as an engineer in the Krupp shipyard in his home city of Kiel and subsequently in the Wilhelmshaven shipyards. Nothing else is known of his life, other than that he survived World War II.

div.head: Ulrich Oppel and Schenker
div.p: Ulrich is frequently mentioned throughout Reinhard Oppel’s correspondence with Schenker. He himself occasionally signed a letter or postcard with his name and the sobriquet “Panzerkreuzer” (battle cruiser). A photograph of him with his two siblings is preserved at OJ 72/11, item 4c (May 1921). He himself was a keen photographer and took many photographs of Heinrich, Jeanette, and the mountain scenery around Galtür – where he, his father, and Walter stayed for a few days in the company of the Schenkers in at least two summers.
